---
title: "Deviance bias under congenial multiple imputation: a sourced derivation"
subtitle: "Canonical formal derivation for the JAIGP theory paper (notation, ledger, proof)"
author: "mi-spectral"
date: last-modified
bibliography: ../literature/mi-spectral-better-bibTeX.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    html-math-method: mathjax
    grid:
      margin-width: 350px
    embed-resources: true
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
execute:
  warning: false
  cache: false
---

## Purpose and how to read this {#sec-purpose}

This is the canonical, human-readable derivation of the Q-function deviance-bias
decomposition — the source of truth for the theory, which the manuscript §3
distills and the companion MI-IC paper cites.

**Verification artifacts referenced below.** The *numerical witness* is a
pre-registered Monte-Carlo simulation (`verification/`) that estimates the bias terms
$\mathbb{E}[A],\mathbb{E}[B],\mathbb{E}[T]$ across configurations and compares them to the
analytic $\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$. The *fixture* is a single simulated dataset
used for an exact, replicate-level cross-check. The *symbolic verification*
(@sec-verify, executed inline) checks the trace identities with Sympy and the MVN
instantiation numerically against the load-bearing R code. A step so verified is
flagged `[mc]` (machine-checked), linking to the appendix code listing that checks it.

**Layout.** The main column carries the mathematics: each definition, its
equation, and a citation. **Provenance lives in the right margin** — the source's
own equation/theorem number, the reproduced source equation, the
`literature/<citekey>.md` line number with a short verbatim snippet (so the
pointer survives re-pagination by `lit-sync`), and any our-notation-vs-source
mapping. A reader follows the math in the column and audits in the margin.

**Status flags.** `[cited]` — established in a source we hold and have read
in-session; `[mc]` — machine-checked in @sec-verify (links to the specific listing);
`[derived]` — proved here from earlier entries.

**Document stages.** (1) Notation and quantities; (2) the ledger (regularity
conditions, algebraic axioms, lemmas); (3) the tagged derivation.

---

## Stage 1 — Notation and quantities {#sec-notation}

Each entry is a *definition* (the D-layer). Facts that are *assumed or invoked*
(regularity, algebra, lemmas) live in the ledger (Stage 2), not here.

### Data, model, and missingness {#sec-data}

**D1 (data and model).** Complete data $Y \in \mathbb{R}^{N\times p}$ with rows
i.i.d. $Y_i \sim \mathcal{N}_p(\mu,\Sigma)$, $\Sigma \succ 0$. Parameter
$\theta = (\mu, \operatorname{vech}\Sigma)$ of dimension $k = p + p(p+1)/2$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Convention.** Complete data $Y=(Y_{\text{obs}},Y_{\text{mis}})$ follows
@schaferAnalysisIncompleteMultivariate1997, <a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:1477</code></a>, where $\ell(\theta\mid Y)$,
$\ell(\theta\mid Y_{\text{obs}})$, $P(Y_{\text{mis}}\mid Y_{\text{obs}},\theta)$ appear
and @littleStatisticalAnalysisMissing2019, <a href="#sec-src-little"><code>:438</code></a>. Meng's congeniality framework writes
$Z_c=\{X,Y_{\text{inc}},I\}$, $Z_o=\{X,Y_{\text{obs}},I,R_{\text{inc}}\}$ with $X$ =
always-observed covariates [@mengMultipleImputationInferencesUncongenial1994a], <a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:95</code></a>
— a different $X$, which is why we avoid the symbol. The verification code
(`verification/00-setup.R`) labels the realized complete matrix `X`; that is our $Y$.
:::

**D2 (missingness).** Response indicator $R$ ($1=$ missing); each row splits the
complete data into observed and missing parts, $Y=(Y_{\text{obs}},Y_{\text{mis}})$.
We assume MAR + distinctness (ignorability); the formal conditions are ledger
R-entries, not definitions.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @rubinInferenceMissingData1976b, <a href="#sec-src-rubin1976"><code>:39</code></a> and @littleStatisticalAnalysisMissing2019, <a href="#sec-src-little"><code>:3263</code></a>: ignorability (MAR + parameter distinctness).
:::

### Likelihoods and estimators {#sec-likelihoods}

**D3 (complete-data log-likelihood and MLE).**
$\ell_{\text{com}}(\theta; Y) = \sum_i \log f_{\mathcal{N}}(Y_i; \mu,\Sigma)$;
$\hat\theta_{\text{com}} = \arg\max_\theta \ell_{\text{com}}(\theta; Y)$.

**D4 (observed-data log-likelihood and MLE).**
$\ell_{\text{obs}}(\theta; Y_{\text{obs}}) = \sum_i \log f_{\mathcal{N}}(Y_{i,\text{obs}}; \mu_{O_i},\Sigma_{O_iO_i})$;
$\hat\theta_{\text{obs}} = \arg\max_\theta \ell_{\text{obs}}$ (the FIML estimator).

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @schaferAnalysisIncompleteMultivariate1997, <a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:1510</code></a>: the observed-data MLE is the maximizer of $\ell(\theta\mid Y_{\text{obs}})$ — "$\hat\theta$ is now the observed-data MLE, the maximizer of $l(\theta\mid Y_{obs})$".
:::

### Information matrices {#sec-information}

**D5 (complete information).** The complete-data Fisher information,
$$ I_{\text{com}}(\theta) = -\,\mathbb{E}_{Y\sim\theta}\!\big[\partial^2\ell_{\text{com}}(\theta;Y)/\partial\theta\,\partial\theta^\top\big] = \mathbb{E}\big[S_{\text{com}}(\theta)\,S_{\text{com}}(\theta)^\top\big]. $$

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @vaartAsymptoticStatistics1998a, <a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:2320</code></a>: Fisher information $I_\theta=\mathbb{E}_\theta[\dot\ell_\theta\dot\ell_\theta^\top]$ and curvature $P_\theta\ddot\ell_\theta=-I_\theta$. MVN closed form: D15.
:::

**D6 (observed information).** The observed-data (expected) Fisher information, the
asymptotic precision of $\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}$ (L1),
$$ I_{\text{obs}}(\theta) = -\,\mathbb{E}_{Y_{\text{obs}}\sim\theta}\!\big[\partial^2\ell_{\text{obs}}(\theta;Y_{\text{obs}})/\partial\theta\,\partial\theta^\top\big]. $$

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @schaferAnalysisIncompleteMultivariate1997, <a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:1489</code></a>: observed information $I_o=-\partial^2\ell(\theta\mid Y_{\text{obs}})/\partial\theta^2$; we use its expectation, the Fisher form (@vaartAsymptoticStatistics1998a, <a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:2320</code></a>). For MVN, the pattern-mixture sum (`fisher_info_obs_mvn`).
:::

**D7 (missing information and the information identity).**
$I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\theta) := I_{\text{com}}(\theta) - I_{\text{obs}}(\theta)$,
so
$$ I_{\text{com}}(\theta) = I_{\text{obs}}(\theta) + I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\theta). $$ {#eq-info-identity}
The missing information is positive semidefinite, $I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}\succeq 0$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @schaferAnalysisIncompleteMultivariate1997, missing-information principle: Schafer establishes $I_c=I_o+I_m$ *conditional on* $Y_{\text{obs}}$ via the EM decomposition $Q(\theta\mid\phi)=\ell_{\text{obs}}+H(\theta\mid\phi)$, with $I_c=-\partial^2 Q(\theta\mid\theta)/\partial\theta^2$ [<a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:1483</code></a>], $I_m=-\partial^2 H(\theta\mid\theta)/\partial\theta^2$ [<a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:1495</code></a>], eq (3.19) [<a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:1501</code></a>]. Taking $\mathbb{E}_{Y_{\text{obs}}}$ gives the identity for the Fisher informations D5-D6 (his conditional $I_c,I_o,I_m$ average to our $I_{\text{com}},I_{\text{obs}},I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}$). $I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}\succeq 0$ `[mc]` (min eigenvalue $\approx 0$ on the fixture, @lst-mc-psd).
:::

### The relative increase in variance (RIV) {#sec-riv}

**D8 (RIV — definition).**
$$ \mathrm{RIV}(\theta) := I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}\, I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}. $$ {#eq-riv}

::: {.column-margin}
**Source (name, scalar form).** @rubinMultipleImputationNonresponse1987, <a href="#sec-src-rubin1987"><code>:2194</code></a>: the relative increase in variance due to nonresponse $r_m = (1+m^{-1})B_m/\bar U_m$; with within $\bar U_m=\sum_l U_{*l}/m$ [<a href="#sec-src-rubin1987"><code>:2167</code></a>], between $B_m=\sum_l(\hat Q_{*l}-\bar Q_m)^{t}(\hat Q_{*l}-\bar Q_m)/(m-1)$ [<a href="#sec-src-rubin1987"><code>:2173</code></a>], total $T_m=\bar U_m+(1+m^{-1})B_m$ [<a href="#sec-src-rubin1987"><code>:2179</code></a>].
:::

**Derived form** (used throughout). By D7 ($I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}=I_{\text{com}}-I_{\text{obs}}$),
matrix distributivity, and $I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}I_{\text{obs}}=I_k$,
$$ \mathrm{RIV} = I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}I_{\text{com}} - I_k, \qquad \operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}) = \operatorname{tr}(I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}I_{\text{com}}) - k. $$ {#eq-trriv}

::: {.column-margin}
**Algebra** [ledger A1-A3]. @petersen2008matrix, §1.1: trace linearity $\operatorname{Tr}(A+B)=\operatorname{Tr}A+\operatorname{Tr}B$ [<a href="#sec-src-petersen"><code>:183</code></a>], $\operatorname{tr}(I_k)=k$ from $\operatorname{Tr}(A)=\sum_i A_{ii}$ [<a href="#sec-src-petersen"><code>:179</code></a>], and cyclicity $\operatorname{Tr}(AB)=\operatorname{Tr}(BA)$ [<a href="#sec-src-petersen"><code>:182</code></a>].
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="simple"}
## Identification: RIV equals Rubin's $r_\infty$ `[derived; mc]`
Under congenial proper MI as $M\to\infty$: $\bar U_\infty = I_{\text{com}}^{-1}$
(complete-data MLE variance) and $T_\infty = I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}$ (variance recovery;
ledger lemma), so $B_\infty = I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}-I_{\text{com}}^{-1}$ and
$$ r_\infty = B_\infty \bar U_\infty^{-1} = (I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}-I_{\text{com}}^{-1})I_{\text{com}} = I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}I_{\text{com}} - I_k = \mathrm{RIV}. $$
Confirmed numerically in @lst-mc-fisher.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Disambiguation — RIV is not the EM rate / "fraction of missing information"

Schafer's **fraction of missing information / EM rate matrix** is
$D = I_{\text{com}}^{-1}I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}$
[@schaferAnalysisIncompleteMultivariate1997 eq (3.21), $D = I_c^{-1}(\hat\theta)I_m(\hat\theta)$], <a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:1517</code></a>.
It shares $I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}$ with RIV but uses $I_{\text{com}}^{-1}$ on the left, not
$I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}$. They relate by $D = (I_k+\mathrm{RIV})^{-1}\mathrm{RIV}$ (scalar:
$\gamma = r/(1+r)$). Our headline $\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})=\operatorname{tr}(I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}I_{\text{com}})-k$
lives specifically on the $I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}$ side; a reader who substitutes $D$ gets the wrong quantity.
:::

### The MI Q-function and the deviance-bias split {#sec-qfun}

**D9 (MI Q-function).**
$$ \bar Q_\infty(\theta) := \lim_{M\to\infty}\frac1M\sum_{l=1}^M \ell_{\text{com}}(\theta; Y_{\text{obs}}, Y_{\text{mis}}^{(l)}) = \mathbb{E}_{Y_{\text{mis}}\mid Y_{\text{obs}}}\!\big[\ell_{\text{com}}(\theta)\big], $$
the imputation-averaged complete-data log-likelihood (the EM/Meng Q-function in
the proper-MI limit).

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** Deterministic-FIML evaluation `barQ_fiml_analytic`
(`verification/00-setup.R`), `[mc]` equal to this construction on the fixture (@lst-mc-fiml).
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Notation clash with Rubin's pooled estimand

$\bar Q_\infty$ here is the averaged *log-likelihood*. It is **not** Rubin's
pooled point estimate $\bar Q_m = \sum_l \hat Q_{*l}/m$
[@rubinMultipleImputationNonresponse1987], <a href="#sec-src-rubin1987"><code>:2161</code></a>, which averages complete-data
*estimates*. We reserve $\bar Q_\infty$ for the averaged log-likelihood throughout.
:::

**D10-D12 (deviance-bias terms).** With $T,A,B$ on the log-likelihood scale (the
deviance scale multiplies by 2):
$$
A := \bar Q_\infty(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}) - \ell_{\text{com}}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})
\quad(\text{imputation bias}),
$$
$$
B := \ell_{\text{com}}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}) - \ell_{\text{com}}(\hat\theta_{\text{com}})
\quad(\text{estimation mismatch}),
$$
$$
T := A + B = \bar Q_\infty(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}) - \ell_{\text{com}}(\hat\theta_{\text{com}})
\quad(\text{total deviance bias}).
$$ {#eq-decomp}

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** This work; operationalized in the numerical witness (`verification/`).
The theorem (Stage 3): $\mathbb{E}[T]=+\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$,
$\mathbb{E}[A]=+\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$, $\mathbb{E}[B]=-\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$.
:::

### Congeniality {#sec-congeniality}

**D13 (congeniality).** The imputation and analysis models are congenial in
Meng's sense; we use this to justify the variance-recovery and Q-function
identities.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @mengMultipleImputationInferencesUncongenial1994a, Definitions 1-2: posterior moments match the analyst's procedure — $[\hat\theta(Z_o),U(Z_o)]\simeq[E_f(\theta\mid Z_o),V_f(\theta\mid Z_o)]$ [eq (2.3.1), <a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:103</code></a>] and $[\hat\theta(Z_c),U(Z_c)]\simeq[E_f(\theta\mid Z_c),V_f(\theta\mid Z_c)]$ [eq (2.3.2), <a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:109</code></a>]; Definition 2 adds $f(Y_{\text{mis}}\mid Z_o)=g(Y_{\text{mis}}\mid Z_o,A)$ [eq (2.3.3), <a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:121</code></a>].
:::

### vech and the duplication matrix {#sec-vech}

**D14 (half-vectorization and duplication matrix).** For symmetric $A$ ($n\times n$),
$\operatorname{vech}(A)$ is the $\tfrac12 n(n+1)$-vector of its lower triangle, and the
duplication matrix $D_n$ satisfies
$$ D_n \operatorname{vech}(A) = \operatorname{vec} A, \qquad \operatorname{vech}(A) = (D_n'D_n)^{-1}D_n'\operatorname{vec} A. $$

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @magnusMatrixDifferentialCalculus2019, Ch 3 §15: duplication matrix $D_n\operatorname{vech}(A)=\operatorname{vec}A$ (eq (23)) [<a href="#sec-src-magnus"><code>:1658</code></a>], $\operatorname{vech}$ definition [<a href="#sec-src-magnus"><code>:1652</code></a>], inverse identity $(D_n'(A\otimes A)D_n)^{-1}=(D_n'D_n)^{-1}D_n'(A^{-1}\otimes A^{-1})D_n(D_n'D_n)^{-1}$ [<a href="#sec-src-magnus"><code>:1692</code></a>].
:::

### MVN instantiation of the information matrices {#sec-mvn-info}

**D15 (closed-form MVN information).** On $\theta=(\mu,\operatorname{vech}\Sigma)$,
block-diagonal,
$$ I_{\text{com}}(\theta) = \begin{pmatrix} N\Sigma^{-1} & 0 \\ 0 & \tfrac{N}{2}D_p'(\Sigma^{-1}\otimes\Sigma^{-1})D_p \end{pmatrix}. $$

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @magnusMatrixDifferentialCalculus2019, MVN ML section: information for $\mu$ and $\operatorname{vech}(\Omega)$ as $n\Omega^{-1}$ and $\tfrac{n}{2}D_m'(\Omega^{-1}\otimes\Omega^{-1})D_m$ [<a href="#sec-src-magnus"><code>:1800</code></a>-<a href="#sec-src-magnus"><code>:1813</code></a>] ($\Omega\!\leftrightarrow\!\Sigma$, $m\!\leftrightarrow\!p$, $n\!\leftrightarrow\!N$). $I_{\text{obs}}$ is the pattern-mixture sum of per-pattern marginal informations (`fisher_info_obs_mvn`, `verification/00-setup.R`); `[mc]` against this closed form in @lst-mc-fisher.
:::

---

## Stage 2 — The ledger (R / A / L) {#sec-ledger}

The registry of everything the derivation *assumes or invokes*; Stage 3 tags each
proof step with the entries below. As in Stage 1, the statement and status flag
sit in the column and provenance (source, equation/theorem number, `md:line`)
sits in the margin. Status flags: `[cited]`, `[mc]`, `[derived]`.

### Regularity conditions (R) {#sec-reg}

**R1 (regular model).** `[cited]` Complete-data model differentiable in quadratic
mean, Lipschitz log-density, consistent MLE; differentiation and integration
interchange, so $P_\theta\dot\ell_\theta=0$ (scores mean-zero) and
$P_\theta\ddot\ell_\theta=-I_\theta$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @vaartAsymptoticStatistics1998a, Thm 5.39: regularity conditions [<a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:7388</code></a>] and score identities [<a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:2320</code></a>]; @schaferAnalysisIncompleteMultivariate1997, <a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:1480</code></a>: "sufficient regularity to interchange differentiation and integration".
:::

**R2 (nonsingular information).** `[cited]` $I_{\text{com}},I_{\text{obs}}\succ 0$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @vaartAsymptoticStatistics1998a, §5.5: nonsingular Fisher information $I_{\text{com}},I_{\text{obs}}\succ 0$ (see @sec-src-vaart).
:::

**R3 (ignorability).** `[cited]` MAR with parameters distinct from $\theta$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @rubinInferenceMissingData1976b, <a href="#sec-src-rubin1976"><code>:39</code></a> and @littleStatisticalAnalysisMissing2019, <a href="#sec-src-little"><code>:3263</code></a>: ignorability (MAR + distinct parameters).
:::

**R4 (congeniality).** `[cited]` = D13.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @mengMultipleImputationInferencesUncongenial1994a, Defs 1-2: congeniality [<a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:99</code></a>/<a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:118</code></a>].
:::

**R5 (properness).** `[cited]` Second-moment proper imputation.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @rubinMultipleImputationNonresponse1987 and @mengMultipleImputationInferencesUncongenial1994a, cond. (c), <a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:506</code></a>: second-moment proper imputation.
:::

**R6 (self-efficiency).** `[cited]` No $\lambda$ makes
$\operatorname{MSE}(\lambda\hat\theta(W_o)+(1-\lambda)\hat\theta(W_c))<\operatorname{MSE}(\hat\theta(W_c))$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @mengMultipleImputationInferencesUncongenial1994a, Def 5: self-efficiency [<a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:450</code></a>], cond. (a) [<a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:504</code></a>].
:::

**R7 ($M\to\infty$).** `[cited]` Infinite-imputation idealization ($\bar Q_\infty,T_\infty$);
finite-$M$ corrections are Rubin's.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @rubinMultipleImputationNonresponse1987, §4: infinite-imputation idealization (see @sec-src-rubin1987).
:::

### Algebraic axioms and properties (A) {#sec-alg}

**A1 (trace linearity).** `[cited]` $\operatorname{Tr}(A+B)=\operatorname{Tr}A+\operatorname{Tr}B$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @petersen2008matrix, §1.1: $\operatorname{Tr}(A+B)=\operatorname{Tr}A+\operatorname{Tr}B$ [<a href="#sec-src-petersen"><code>:183</code></a>].
:::

**A2 (trace cyclicity).** `[cited]` $\operatorname{Tr}(AB)=\operatorname{Tr}(BA)$, $\operatorname{Tr}(ABC)=\operatorname{Tr}(BCA)$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @petersen2008matrix, §1.1: $\operatorname{Tr}(AB)=\operatorname{Tr}(BA)$ [<a href="#sec-src-petersen"><code>:182</code></a>], $\operatorname{Tr}(ABC)=\operatorname{Tr}(BCA)$ [<a href="#sec-src-petersen"><code>:184</code></a>].
:::

**A3 ($\operatorname{tr}I_k=k$).** `[cited]` From $\operatorname{Tr}(A)=\sum_i A_{ii}$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @petersen2008matrix, §1.1: $\operatorname{Tr}(A)=\sum_i A_{ii}$ [<a href="#sec-src-petersen"><code>:179</code></a>].
:::

**A4 (vec / Kronecker).** `[cited]` $\operatorname{vec}(AXB)=(B^\top\!\otimes A)\operatorname{vec}X$;
$\operatorname{Tr}(A^\top B)=\operatorname{vec}(A)^\top\operatorname{vec}(B)$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @petersen2008matrix, §10.2.2: $\operatorname{vec}(AXB)=(B^\top\!\otimes A)\operatorname{vec}X$ (eq (520)) [<a href="#sec-src-petersen"><code>:3345</code></a>], $\operatorname{Tr}(A^\top B)=\operatorname{vec}(A)^\top\operatorname{vec}(B)$ (eq (521)) [<a href="#sec-src-petersen"><code>:3346</code></a>].
:::

**A5 (duplication matrix).** `[cited]` = D14; plus
$(D_n'(A\otimes A)D_n)^{-1}=(D_n'D_n)^{-1}D_n'(A^{-1}\!\otimes A^{-1})D_n(D_n'D_n)^{-1}$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @magnusMatrixDifferentialCalculus2019, §15: duplication matrix [<a href="#sec-src-magnus"><code>:1658</code></a>] and its inverse identity [<a href="#sec-src-magnus"><code>:1692</code></a>].
:::

**A6 (symmetry / inverse-transpose).** `[cited]` Information matrices are symmetric,
$(A^{-1})^\top=(A^\top)^{-1}$, $\operatorname{Tr}(A)=\operatorname{Tr}(A^\top)$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @petersen2008matrix, §1.1: $\operatorname{Tr}(A)=\operatorname{Tr}(A^\top)$ [<a href="#sec-src-petersen"><code>:181</code></a>]. (The symmetry `symmetric_reduce` enforces; see @sec-verify-tracenorm.)
:::

**A7 (Gaussian conditional / Schur complement).** `[cited]` For $X\sim N(\mu,\Sigma)$
partitioned $(X^{(1)},X^{(2)})$, the conditional $X^{(1)}\mid X^{(2)}=x^{(2)}$ is normal
with mean $\mu^{(1)}+\Sigma_{12}\Sigma_{22}^{-1}(x^{(2)}-\mu^{(2)})$ and covariance
$\Sigma_{11\cdot 2}=\Sigma_{11}-\Sigma_{12}\Sigma_{22}^{-1}\Sigma_{21}$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @andersonIntroductionMultivariateStatistical2003, §2.5.1: Gaussian conditional / Schur complement [<a href="#sec-src-anderson"><code>:1582</code></a>/<a href="#sec-src-anderson"><code>:1595</code></a>]. (Term A's FIML Q-function; matches `barQ_fiml_analytic`'s `cond_V`.)
:::

**A8 (expectation identities).** `[cited]` Linearity of $\mathbb{E}$; iterated expectation
$\mathbb{E}[\mathbb{E}(X\mid Y)]=\mathbb{E}X$; conditional expectation as $L^2$ projection
(residual orthogonality); quadratic-form expectation
$\mathbb{E}[v^\top M v]=\operatorname{tr}(M\,\mathbb{E}[vv^\top])$ (via A2 + linearity).

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @vaartAsymptoticStatistics1998a, Ch 11: conditional expectation as $L^2$ projection [<a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:5147</code></a>/<a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:5161</code></a>/<a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:5170</code></a>].
:::

**A9 (second-order Taylor).** `[cited]` $f(\theta_1)-f(\theta_0)=\nabla f(\theta_0)^\top\delta+\tfrac12\delta^\top\nabla^2 f(\theta_0)\delta+o(\|\delta\|^2)$, $\delta=\theta_1-\theta_0$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @vaartAsymptoticStatistics1998a, §5.5: score linearization (see @sec-src-vaart).
:::

### Lemmas (L) {#sec-lem}

**L1 (MLE asymptotic linearity and normality).** `[cited]` Under R1-R2,
$$ \sqrt{n}(\hat\theta_n-\theta_0)=\tfrac{1}{\sqrt n}\textstyle\sum_i I_{\theta_0}^{-1}\dot\ell_{\theta_0}(X_i)+o_P(1)\;\rightsquigarrow\;N(0,I_{\theta_0}^{-1}), $$
hence $\operatorname{Var}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})=I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}$, $\operatorname{Var}(\hat\theta_{\text{com}})=I_{\text{com}}^{-1}$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @vaartAsymptoticStatistics1998a, Thm 5.39: linear expansion [<a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:7388</code></a>/<a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:7393</code></a>], covariance $I_\theta^{-1}$ [<a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:4170</code></a>].
:::

**L3 (Fisher/Louis score identity).** `[cited+derived]` Differentiating the loglik
decomposition $\ell(\theta\mid Y)=\ell(\theta\mid Y_{\text{obs}})+\log P(Y_{\text{mis}}\mid Y_{\text{obs}},\theta)+c$
once and taking $\mathbb{E}[\cdot\mid Y_{\text{obs}}]$ (conditional score mean-zero, R1) gives
$U_{\text{obs}}=\mathbb{E}[U_{\text{com}}\mid Y_{\text{obs}}]$; hence by A8,
$\operatorname{Cov}(U_{\text{obs}},U_{\text{com}})=\operatorname{Var}(U_{\text{obs}})=I_{\text{obs}}$.

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @schaferAnalysisIncompleteMultivariate1997, loglik decomposition: $\ell(\theta\mid Y)=\ell_{\text{obs}}+\log P$ [<a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:944</code></a>], $Q/H$ [<a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:951</code></a>/<a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:964</code></a>], 2nd-derivative analog (3.19) [<a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:1501</code></a>]; @vaartAsymptoticStatistics1998a, <a href="#sec-src-vaart"><code>:2320</code></a>: score-mean-zero.
:::

**L2 (variance of the estimator gap).** `[derived; mc]`
$$ \operatorname{Var}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}-\hat\theta_{\text{com}}) = I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}-I_{\text{com}}^{-1}. $$ {#eq-varGap}
*Proof.* By L1, $\hat\theta_\bullet-\theta_0\approx I_\bullet^{-1}U_\bullet$; by L3,
$\operatorname{Cov}(U_{\text{obs}},U_{\text{com}})=I_{\text{obs}}$, so
$\operatorname{Cov}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}},\hat\theta_{\text{com}})=I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}I_{\text{obs}}I_{\text{com}}^{-1}=I_{\text{com}}^{-1}$;
then $\operatorname{Var}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}-\hat\theta_{\text{com}})=I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}-I_{\text{com}}^{-1}-I_{\text{com}}^{-1}+I_{\text{com}}^{-1}$.
**This is the lemma whose misuse — substituting $\operatorname{Var}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})$ for
$\operatorname{Var}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}-\hat\theta_{\text{com}})$ — produces the spurious
$-\tfrac12 k$ (see D8 disambiguation and Term B).**

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** `[derived]` from L1, L3, A8; verified symbolically (Tier B, @lst-mc-tierb).
:::

**L4 (MI variance recovery).** `[cited]` Under R4-R7, $\bar\theta_\infty$ is consistent
and $T_\infty=\operatorname{Var}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})=I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}$. (Anchors the D8 RIV
identification.)

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @mengMultipleImputationInferencesUncongenial1994a, Lemma 3 [<a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:486</code></a>] and Main result [<a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:503</code></a>/<a href="#sec-src-meng"><code>:511</code></a>]: variance recovery; also @rubinMultipleImputationNonresponse1987, §4 (see @sec-src-rubin1987).
:::

## Stage 3 — The tagged derivation {#sec-derivation}

Each step cites the ledger entries it invokes in `[...]`. `[mc]` marks a step
machine-checked in @sec-verify. Let $\theta_0$ denote the
data-generating parameter, and $\tilde\phi$ the imputation parameter — $\phi$ (not
$\theta$) marks it as the imputer's parameter, distinct from the analysis parameter
even where the two spaces coincide under congeniality, and the tilde marks a
posterior *draw* (it collapses to the point value $\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}$ under FIML).
$S,\, \mathcal{J}$ denote the score and observed information. The detailed second-order
bookkeeping follows the companion MI-IC derivation, translated here to
general-likelihood notation.

### Theorem {#sec-thm}

**Theorem (Q-function deviance bias).** Under R1-R7, to leading order
$$ \mathbb{E}[T] = \mathbb{E}\big[\bar Q_\infty(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}) - \ell_{\text{com}}(\hat\theta_{\text{com}};Y)\big] = +\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}) + O(n^{-1}), $$ {#eq-theorem}
decomposing (D10-D12) as $\mathbb{E}[A]=+\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})+O(n^{-1})$ (imputation
bias) and $\mathbb{E}[B]=-\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})+O(n^{-1})$ (estimation
mismatch). The deviance-scale statement multiplies by 2.

### Term B $=-\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ (estimation mismatch) {#sec-termB}

This term is elementary and exact to leading order.

**B1.** $\hat\theta_{\text{com}}=\arg\max_\theta\ell_{\text{com}}(\theta;Y)$ [D3], so
$\ell_{\text{com}}(\hat\theta_{\text{com}})\ge\ell_{\text{com}}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})$ and $B\le 0$.

**B2.** Second-order Taylor of $\ell_{\text{com}}$ about its maximizer $\hat\theta_{\text{com}}$ [A9];
the first-order term vanishes since $S_{\text{com}}(\hat\theta_{\text{com}})=0$ [D3]:
$$ B = -\tfrac12(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}-\hat\theta_{\text{com}})^\top \mathcal{J}_{\text{com}}(\hat\theta_{\text{com}})(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}-\hat\theta_{\text{com}}) + O_p(n^{-3/2}). $$

**B3.** Take $\mathbb{E}$; replace $\mathcal{J}_{\text{com}}(\hat\theta_{\text{com}})\to_p I_{\text{com}}$ [R1, R2];
apply $a^\top Ba=\operatorname{tr}(Baa^\top)$ [A8, A2]; both estimators are
asymptotically unbiased for $\theta_0$, so $\mathbb{E}[\cdot\,(\cdot)^\top]$ is the variance of the gap:
$$ \mathbb{E}[B] = -\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}\!\big(I_{\text{com}}\operatorname{Var}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}-\hat\theta_{\text{com}})\big)+O(n^{-3/2}). $$

**B4.** Substitute L2, then reduce the trace:
$$ \mathbb{E}[B] = -\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}\!\big(I_{\text{com}}(I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}-I_{\text{com}}^{-1})\big)\ \text{[L2]}\ = -\tfrac12\big(\operatorname{tr}(I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}I_{\text{com}})-k\big)\ \text{[A1, A2, A3]}\ = -\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})\ \text{[D8]}. $$ {#eq-termB}

::: {.column-margin}
`[mc]` @lst-mc-termb (`B + tr(RIV)/2 == 0`).
:::

### Term A $=+\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ (imputation bias) {#sec-termA}

**A1.** Factor the complete-data log-likelihood
$\ell_{\text{com}}(\theta;Y)=\ell_{\text{obs}}(\theta;Y_{\text{obs}})+\ell_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\theta)$,
$\ell_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\theta):=\log P(Y_{\text{mis}}\mid Y_{\text{obs}},\theta)$.
The $\ell_{\text{obs}}$ parts cancel in $A$ (D9, D10), leaving

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** @schaferAnalysisIncompleteMultivariate1997, eq (3.2): complete-data factorization [<a href="#sec-src-schafer"><code>:944</code></a>].
:::
$$ A = \mathbb{E}\big[\,\mathbb{E}_{Y_{\text{mis}}\mid Y_{\text{obs}},\tilde\phi}[\ell_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})] - \ell_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})\,\big]. $$

**A2.** By iterated expectation over the true completion $Y_{\text{mis}}\sim\theta_0$ [A8],
$$ A = \mathbb{E}_{Y_{\text{obs}}}\big[\,\mathbb{E}_{\tilde\phi}[\ell_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})] - \mathbb{E}_{\theta_0}[\ell_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})]\,\big], $$
the discrepancy between averaging the missing-data log-density under $\tilde\phi$ versus the truth $\theta_0$.

**A3.** Second-order Taylor in $\tilde\phi$ about $\theta_0$, using the score-function
identity $\partial_\phi\mathbb{E}_\phi[g]\big|_{\theta_0}=\mathbb{E}_{\theta_0}[g\,S_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}]$
[R1, differentiate under the integral] and Bartlett
$\mathbb{E}_{\theta_0}[S_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}S_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}^\top]=I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}$
[R1, D7], then expanding $\ell_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})$ about $\theta_0$, gives three pieces:
$$ A = \underbrace{\mathbb{E}[(\tilde\phi-\theta_0)^\top\alpha]}_{(A)} + \underbrace{\mathbb{E}[(\tilde\phi-\theta_0)^\top I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}-\theta_0)]}_{(B)\ \text{main}} + \underbrace{\tfrac12\mathbb{E}[(\tilde\phi-\theta_0)^\top H_{\phi\phi}(\tilde\phi-\theta_0)]}_{(C)}. $$

::: {.callout-warning}
## $\alpha$ is not zero (do not invoke Gibbs)
$\alpha = \mathbb{E}_{\theta_0}[\ell_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\theta_0)\,S_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}(\theta_0)] = \operatorname{Cov}_{\theta_0}(\ell_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}},S_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}) = -\mathcal{E}'(\theta_0)$,
the gradient of the conditional entropy — generally nonzero (e.g. $N(\mu,\sigma^2)$:
$\alpha_\mu=0$, $\alpha_{\sigma^2}=-n_{\text{mis}}/2\sigma^2\neq 0$). Gibbs' inequality
concerns varying the *evaluation* point of $\mathbb{E}_{\theta_0}[\log p_\theta]$; $\alpha$
varies the *sampling* distribution. The two are different functions — an earlier
shortcut that set $\alpha=0$ was wrong (v4.1 correction).
:::

**A4 (cancellation).** Pieces $(A)$ and $(C)$ are each $O(1)$ but their leading
contributions cancel — $(A)$ pairs the bias of $\tilde\phi$ with the entropy
gradient, $(C)$ the curvature of the conditional cross-entropy with $\operatorname{Var}(\tilde\phi)$,
linked through the information geometry of $P(Y_{\text{mis}}\mid Y_{\text{obs}},\theta)$:
$$ (A)+(C) = O(n^{-1}). $$
To leading order only the main piece $(B)$ survives:
$A = \operatorname{tr}\!\big(I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}\operatorname{Cov}(\tilde\phi,\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})\big)+O(n^{-1})$ [A2, A8].

::: {.column-margin}
**Source.** $(A)+(C)=O(n^{-1})$ verified exactly in the normal and exponential
models — companion MI-IC derivation.
:::

**A5.** Under congenial proper imputation the imputation parameter satisfies
$\tilde\phi=\hat\theta_{\text{obs}}+\Delta\tilde\phi$ with $\operatorname{Cov}(\Delta\tilde\phi,\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})=0$ [R4, R5],
so $\operatorname{Cov}(\tilde\phi,\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})=\operatorname{Var}(\hat\theta_{\text{obs}})=I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}$ [L1].
Hence
$$ \mathbb{E}[A] = \operatorname{tr}\!\big(I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}\,I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}\big) + O(n^{-1}) = \operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}) + O(n^{-1})\ \text{[D7, D8]}. $$ {#eq-termA}

::: {.column-margin}
`[mc]` @lst-mc-terma (`A - tr(RIV) == 0`) for the trace
identity; the MVN FIML instantiation of $\bar Q_\infty$ matches the R code to machine
precision (@lst-mc-fiml).
:::

### Combine: $T = A + B = +\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ {#sec-combine}

$$ \mathbb{E}[T] = \mathbb{E}[A]+\mathbb{E}[B] = \operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}) - \tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}) = +\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}) + O(n^{-1}) $$ {#eq-combine}
[D10-D12], combining the Term A (@eq-termA) and Term B (@eq-termB) results. $\qquad\blacksquare$

::: {.column-margin}
`[mc]` @lst-mc-combine (`T - tr(RIV)/2 == 0`).
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Leading order, finite-sample residual, and the verification stack
The identities $\mathbb{E}[A]=+\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ and $\mathbb{E}[B]=-\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$
hold *to leading order*; each carries an $O(n^{-1})$ correction (Term A's from the
$(A)+(C)$ cancellation in A4). These per-term residuals are real at finite $n$ —
the numerical witness sees the individual terms $\sim$10-20% off while their **sum**
$\mathbb{E}[T]=+\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ holds within Monte-Carlo error, because the
residuals cancel. The rigor stack: this tagged derivation (algebra), the inline symbolic
verification (@sec-verify; trace identities + MVN instantiation to machine precision), and the
pre-registered numerical witness (12 cells, two engines).
:::

---

## Appendix A — Verbatim source passages {#sec-sources}

Consolidated provenance audit, grouped by reference. Each entry reproduces — verbatim
from the `literature/<citekey>.md` Mathpix conversions — the specific passages the
derivation invokes, tagged with the source's own equation/theorem number and the
`literature/<citekey>.md` line. The margin provenance blocks in Stages 1-3 keep a short
snippet as a re-pagination-stable anchor and link here (`@sec-src-<key>`) for the full
text.

**Fair-use flag (not a blocker).** The excerpts below are short equation/definition
fragments reproduced for an internal audit document. For the JAIGP submission, keep them
to minimal equation/definition excerpts (fair use); if the venue or a reviewer objects to
verbatim reproduction, trim each entry to its equation/theorem number plus line reference
and drop the quoted text.

### Schafer (1997) — Analysis of Incomplete Multivariate Data {#sec-src-schafer}

[@schaferAnalysisIncompleteMultivariate1997]. Invoked by D1, D4, D6, D7, R1, L3, Term A step A1.

**Complete-data likelihood factorization, eq (3.2)** [`:944`].

> $l(\theta \mid Y)=l\left(\theta \mid Y_{obs}\right)+\log P\left(Y_{mis} \mid Y_{obs}, \theta\right)+c$

where (line 947) "$l(\theta/Y)=\log P(Y \mid \theta)$ denotes the complete-data log
likelihood, $l(\theta/Y_{obs})$ ... the observed-data loglikelihood, and $c$ an arbitrary
constant."

**EM decomposition of the Q-function** [`:952`, `:958`, `:964`].

> $Q\left(\theta \mid \theta^{(t)}\right)=l\left(\theta \mid Y_{obs}\right)+H\left(\theta \mid \theta^{(t)}\right)+c$

with $Q(\theta\mid\theta^{(t)})=\int l(\theta\mid Y)\,P(Y_{mis}\mid Y_{obs},\theta^{(t)})\,dY_{mis}$
and $H(\theta\mid\theta^{(t)})=\int \log P(Y_{mis}\mid Y_{obs},\theta)\,P(Y_{mis}\mid Y_{obs},\theta^{(t)})\,dY_{mis}$.

**Missing-information principle and the information identity, eq (3.19)** [`:1480`-`:1501`].

> (line 1480) "the complete information is equal to the observed information plus the
> missing information ... called the missing information principle by Orchard and Woodbury
> (1972)"

with $I_c(\theta)=-\partial^2 Q(\theta\mid\theta)/\partial\theta^2$ [`:1483`],
$I_o(\theta)=-\partial^2 l(\theta\mid Y_{obs})/\partial\theta^2$ [`:1489`],
$I_m(\theta)=-\partial^2 H(\theta\mid\theta)/\partial\theta^2$ [`:1495`], so

> $I_c(\theta)=I_o(\theta)+I_m(\theta)$ &nbsp; (3.19) [`:1501`]

**Observed-data MLE** [`:1510`].

> "where $\hat\theta$ is now the observed-data MLE, the maximizer of $l(\theta\mid Y_{obs})$."

**EM rate matrix (fraction of missing information), eq (3.21)** [`:1517`] — cited for the
D8 disambiguation; this is *not* RIV.

> $D=I_c^{-1}(\hat\theta)\, I_m(\hat\theta)$

### Magnus & Neudecker (2019) — Matrix Differential Calculus {#sec-src-magnus}

[@magnusMatrixDifferentialCalculus2019]. Invoked by D14, D15, A5.

**Half-vectorization** [`:1652`] (here for $3\times3$ symmetric $A$).

> $\operatorname{vech}(A)=\left(a_{11}, a_{21}, a_{31}, a_{22}, a_{32}, a_{33}\right)^{\prime}$

**Duplication matrix, eq (23)** [`:1658`], and its inverse [`:1664`].

> $D_{n} \operatorname{vech}(A)=\operatorname{vec} A \quad\left(A=A^{\prime}\right)$

> $\operatorname{vech}(A)=\left(D_{n}^{\prime} D_{n}\right)^{-1} D_{n}^{\prime} \operatorname{vec} A \quad\left(A=A^{\prime}\right)$

**Inverse of $D_n'(A\otimes A)D_n$** [`:1692`].

> $\left(D_{n}^{\prime}(A \otimes A) D_{n}\right)^{-1}=\left(D_{n}^{\prime} D_{n}\right)^{-1} D_{n}^{\prime}\left(A^{-1} \otimes A^{-1}\right) D_{n}\left(D_{n}^{\prime} D_{n}\right)^{-1}$

**MVN information matrix for $(\mu,\operatorname{vech}\Omega)$** [`:1804`-`:1811`]
($\Omega\!\leftrightarrow\!\Sigma$, $m\!\leftrightarrow\!p$, $n\!\leftrightarrow\!N$ in our D15).

> $\mathcal{F}=n\begin{pmatrix}\Omega^{-1} & 0 \\ 0 & \frac{1}{2} D_{m}^{\prime}\left(\Omega^{-1} \otimes \Omega^{-1}\right) D_{m}\end{pmatrix}$

### van der Vaart (1998) — Asymptotic Statistics {#sec-src-vaart}

[@vaartAsymptoticStatistics1998a]. Invoked by D5, D6, R1, R2, A8, A9, L1, L3.

**Score identities** [`:2320`].

> "$P_{\theta} \dot{\ell}_{\theta}=0$ (scores have mean zero), and $P_{\theta} \ddot{\ell}_{\theta}=-I_{\theta}$ (the curvature of the likelihood is equal to minus the Fisher information)."

**MLE asymptotic normality** [`:2325`].

> $\sqrt{n}\left(\hat{\theta}_{n}-\theta\right) \rightsquigarrow N\left(0, I_{\theta}^{-1}\right)$

**MLE linear (influence-function) expansion, Theorem 5.39** [`:4170`, `:7388`-`:7393`];
the joint full/null-restricted form at `:7392`-`:7393` underpins L3's score covariance.

> $\sqrt{n}\left(\hat{\theta}_{n}-\theta\right)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{n}} \sum_{i=1}^{n} I_{\theta}^{-1} \dot{\ell}_{\theta}\left(X_{i}\right)+o_{P_{\theta}}(1)$

**Conditional expectation as $L^2$ projection (Ch 11)** [`:5161`, `:5170`].

> (line 5161) "the conditional expectation is the unique measurable function $E(X \mid Y)$ of $Y$ that satisfies the orthogonality relation $\mathrm{E}(X-\mathrm{E}(X \mid Y)) g(Y)=0$"; (line 5170, Example 11.3) "$\mathrm{EX}=E E(X \mid Y)$ ... 'the expectation of a conditional expectation is the expectation.'"

### Meng (1994) — Multiple-Imputation Inferences with Uncongenial Sources of Input {#sec-src-meng}

[@mengMultipleImputationInferencesUncongenial1994a]. Invoked by D1, D13, R4, R5, R6, L4.
Complete data $Z_{c}=\{X, Y_{inc}, I\}$, observed data $Z_{o}=\{X, Y_{obs}, I, R_{inc}\}$ [`:95`].

**Definition 1 (congeniality), eqs (2.3.1)-(2.3.2)** [`:102`-`:110`].

> $\left[\widehat{\theta}\left(Z_{o}\right), U\left(Z_{o}\right)\right] \simeq\left[E_{f}\left[\theta \mid Z_{o}\right], V_{f}\left[\theta \mid Z_{o}\right]\right]$ &nbsp; (2.3.1)

> $\left[\widehat{\theta}\left(Z_{c}\right), U\left(Z_{c}\right)\right] \simeq\left[E_{f}\left[\theta \mid Z_{c}\right], V_{f}\left[\theta \mid Z_{c}\right]\right]$ &nbsp; (2.3.2)

**Definition 2 (congeniality to an imputation model), eq (2.3.3)** [`:120`].

> $f\left(Y_{mis} \mid Z_{o}\right)=g\left(Y_{mis} \mid Z_{o}, A\right)$

**Definition 5 (self-efficiency)** [`:450`].

> "A statistical estimation procedure $\widehat{\theta}(\cdot)$ ... is said to be self-efficient ... if there is no $\lambda \in(-\infty, \infty)$ such that the mean-squared error of $\lambda \hat{\theta}\left(W_{o}\right)+(1-\lambda) \hat{\theta}\left(W_{c}\right)$ is less than that of $\widehat{\theta}\left(W_{c}\right)$."

**Lemma 3 and Main result (variance recovery / efficiency)** [`:486`, `:503`-`:511`]; conditions
(a) self-efficiency and (c) "second-moment proper" imputation [`:504`, `:506`].

> "(i) The repeated-imputation estimator is consistent for $\theta$, and is at least as efficient as the analyst's incomplete-data estimator."

### Rubin (1987) — Multiple Imputation for Nonresponse in Surveys {#sec-src-rubin1987}

[@rubinMultipleImputationNonresponse1987]. Invoked by D8, R5, R7, L4, and the D9 notation-clash callout.

**Pooled estimate and the variance components** [`:2161`, `:2167`, `:2173`, `:2179`].

> $\bar{Q}_{m}=\sum_{l=1}^{m} \hat{Q}_{* l} / m$ ; &nbsp; $\bar{U}_{m}=\sum_{l=1}^{m} U_{* l} / m$ ; &nbsp; $B_{m}=\sum_{l=1}^{m}\left(\hat{Q}_{* l}-\bar{Q}_{m}\right)^{t}\left(\hat{Q}_{* l}-\bar{Q}_{m}\right) /(m-1)$ ; &nbsp; $T_{m}=\bar{U}_{m}+\left(1+m^{-1}\right) B_{m}$

**Relative increase in variance due to nonresponse** [`:2194`] (named at `:2191`).

> $r_{m}=\left(1+m^{-1}\right) B_{m} / \bar{U}_{m}$

### Rubin (1976) — Inference and Missing Data {#sec-src-rubin1976}

[@rubinInferenceMissingData1976b]. Invoked by D2, R3 (ignorability).

**Ignorability for likelihood / Bayesian inference (abstract)** [`:39`].

> "When making direct-likelihood or Bayesian inferences about $\theta$, it is appropriate to ignore the process that causes missing data if the missing data are missing at random and the parameter of the missing data process is 'distinct' from $\theta$."

### Little & Rubin (2019) — Statistical Analysis with Missing Data, 3rd ed. {#sec-src-little}

[@littleStatisticalAnalysisMissing2019]. Invoked by D1, D2, R3.

**MAR definition** [`:438`-`:444`].

> "missingness depends on $y_{i}$ only through the observed components $y_{(0)i}$ ... The missingness mechanism is then called missing at random (MAR)."

**Ignorability — Definition 6.4 and Theorem 6.1A(a)** [`:3263`, `:3277`].

> (Def 6.4) "The missingness mechanism is called ignorable if inferences for $\theta$ based on the ignorable likelihood ... are the same as inferences ... based on the full likelihood"; (Thm 6.1A(a), parameter distinctness) "the joint parameter space $(\theta, \psi)$ ... is the product of the parameter space $\Omega_{\theta}$ of $\theta$ and the parameter space $\Omega_{\psi}$ of $\psi$."

### Anderson (2003) — An Introduction to Multivariate Statistical Analysis {#sec-src-anderson}

[@andersonIntroductionMultivariateStatistical2003]. Invoked by A7 (Gaussian conditional / Schur complement).

**Conditional covariance (Schur complement)** [`:1582`].

> $\Sigma_{11 \cdot 2}=\Sigma_{11}-\Sigma_{12} \Sigma_{22}^{-1} \Sigma_{21}$

**Conditional mean** [`:1598`]; the $q$-variate normal conditional density $f(x^{(1)} \mid x^{(2)})$ has this mean and covariance $\Sigma_{11\cdot2}$ [`:1595`].

> $\mathscr{E}\left(X^{(1)} \mid x^{(2)}\right)=\mu^{(1)}+\Sigma_{12} \Sigma_{22}^{-1}\left(x^{(2)}-\mu^{(2)}\right)$

### Petersen & Pedersen — The Matrix Cookbook {#sec-src-petersen}

[@petersen2008matrix]. Invoked by A1-A4, A6, and the @eq-trriv trace reduction.

**§1.1 Trace** [`:177`-`:187`]. In our Mathpix conversion this section is a single
unnumbered aligned display, so the Cookbook's own inline equation numbers are **not**
recoverable from the file; these trace identities are therefore cited by section + line.

> $\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{A})=\sum_{i} A_{ii}$ [`:179`]; &nbsp; $\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{A})=\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{A}^{T})$ [`:181`]; &nbsp; $\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{A B})=\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{B A})$ [`:182`]; &nbsp; $\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{A}+\mathbf{B})=\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{A})+\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{B})$ [`:183`]; &nbsp; $\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{A B C})=\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{B C A})=\operatorname{Tr}(\mathbf{C A B})$ [`:184`]

**§10.2.2 vec-operator, eqs (520)-(521)** [`:3345`-`:3346`]. Equation numbers confirmed
in-file: the vec rule is cited as "Eq 520" at `:3692`, and "Eq. 524" closes the same
numbered block at `:3353`.

> $\operatorname{vec}(\mathbf{A X B})=\left(\mathbf{B}^{T} \otimes \mathbf{A}\right) \operatorname{vec}(\mathbf{X})$ &nbsp; (520)

> $\operatorname{Tr}\left(\mathbf{A}^{T} \mathbf{B}\right)=\operatorname{vec}(\mathbf{A})^{T} \operatorname{vec}(\mathbf{B})$ &nbsp; (521)

---

## Appendix B — Machine-checked symbolic verification {#sec-verify}

The `[mc]` flags throughout Stages 1-3 link to the executable listings in this
appendix. The code runs inline at render time (Sympy + NumPy); every identity is
checked by reducing `lhs - rhs` to `0` (symbolic) or asserting a match to the
load-bearing R code `verification/00-setup.R` (numeric, machine precision). This is
the algebraic companion to the *numerical* witness W1 (`verification/W1-sweep.R`),
which confirms $\mathbb{E}[T]\approx+\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ at many
concrete configurations; here we check the chain of identities that produces it.

Scope: the trace bookkeeping that combines the asymptotic second-moment inputs into
$+\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ is **symbolic** (exact, parameterization-free);
the MVN closed forms for $I_{\text{com}},I_{\text{obs}},\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$
and the FIML Q-function are reimplemented in NumPy and checked **to machine precision**
against the R code on a fixed replicate; and $\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ is
checked **within Monte-Carlo SE** of the W1 cache $\mathbb{E}[T]$. What Sympy does not
prove are the second-moment inputs themselves (the asymptotic covariances L1-L4); those
are likelihood asymptotics stated as load-bearing lemmas (Stage 2) and validated
end-to-end by W1.

```{python}
import sympy as sp
import numpy as np
from sympy import MatMul, MatAdd, Identity, Trace, Transpose, S
from sympy.matrices.expressions.matexpr import MatrixExpr, MatrixSymbol
from numpy.linalg import inv, slogdet, eigvalsh
from pathlib import Path
import json

print("sympy", sp.__version__, "| numpy", np.__version__)
```

### vech / duplication-matrix machinery {#sec-verify-vech}

MVN running example: $X \sim \mathcal{N}_p(\mu, \Sigma)$, parameter
$\theta = (\mu,\ \operatorname{vech}\Sigma)$ of dimension $k = p + p(p+1)/2$ (D1, D14).
Sympy has no vech/duplication operator, so we build $D_p$ explicitly, mirroring
`00-setup.R` (column-major lower triangle).

```{python}
def vech_index_table(p):
    m = np.zeros((p, p), int); c = 0
    for j in range(p):
        for i in range(j, p):
            c += 1; m[i, j] = c; m[j, i] = c
    return m

def duplication_matrix(p, sym=False):
    kk = p * (p + 1) // 2
    D = sp.zeros(p * p, kk) if sym else np.zeros((p * p, kk))
    c = 0
    for j in range(p):
        for i in range(j, p):
            D[j * p + i, c] = 1
            if i != j:
                D[i * p + j, c] = 1
            c += 1
    return D
```

### The trace normal form (load-bearing helper) {#sec-verify-tracenorm}

Sympy's `Trace` does not auto-apply linearity, does not evaluate
`Trace(Identity) = n`, and does not exploit cyclic invariance `Trace(AB) = Trace(BA)`;
worse, `sympy.simplify` on a `Trace` hits a `signsimp`/`cancel` bug and raises. The
helper below (i) expands matrix products inside each trace, (ii) splits `Trace` over
sums, (iii) evaluates `Trace(c*I) = c*n`, and (iv) canonicalizes cyclic factor order so
cyclically-equal traces collapse. Every symbolic identity is checked after `trace_normal`.

```{python}
def symmetric_reduce(expr):
    """Treat every bare MatrixSymbol as symmetric: Transpose(X) -> X."""
    return expr.replace(
        lambda x: isinstance(x, Transpose) and isinstance(x.arg, MatrixSymbol),
        lambda x: x.arg)

def trace_normal(expr, n):
    """Normal form for scalar Trace(MatMul) expressions (avoids sympy.simplify)."""
    expr = sp.expand(symmetric_reduce(sp.expand(expr, trace=True)), trace=True)

    def handle(t):
        m = symmetric_reduce(sp.expand(t.arg))
        if isinstance(m, MatAdd):
            return sum(handle(Trace(a)) for a in m.args)
        coeff = S.One
        if isinstance(m, MatMul):
            for a in m.args:
                if not isinstance(a, MatrixExpr):
                    coeff *= a
            mats = [a for a in m.args
                    if isinstance(a, MatrixExpr) and not isinstance(a, Identity)]
            if not mats:
                return coeff * n
            rotations = [mats[i:] + mats[:i] for i in range(len(mats))]
            best = min(rotations, key=lambda fs: tuple(sp.srepr(x) for x in fs))
            return coeff * Trace(MatMul(*best))
        if isinstance(m, Identity):
            return coeff * n
        return coeff * Trace(m)

    return expr.replace(lambda x: isinstance(x, Trace), handle)
```

### Term-combination identities (abstract, exact) {#sec-verify-terms}

Treat $I_{\text{com}}, I_{\text{obs}}$ as abstract symmetric positive-definite
$n\times n$ matrices ($n=k$). The asymptotic second-moment inputs (Stage 2 lemmas
L1-L4, proved there, not here) feed the trace bookkeeping. First, `tr(RIV)`
(@eq-trriv) in normal form:

```{python}
#| label: lst-mc-trriv
#| lst-cap: "tr(RIV) = tr(I_obs^-1 I_com) - n, in trace normal form (D8)."
n = sp.Symbol('n', positive=True, integer=True)
I_com = sp.MatrixSymbol('I_com', n, n)
I_obs = sp.MatrixSymbol('I_obs', n, n)
I_mis = I_com - I_obs                      # I_{mis|obs} := I_com - I_obs

trRIV = trace_normal(Trace(I_obs.inv() * I_mis), n)
print("tr(RIV)  =", trRIV)                  # = Trace(I_obs^{-1} I_com) - n
```

**Term A** $= +\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ — plug L1 into L3, then cyclic invariance
(@eq-termA):

```{python}
#| label: lst-mc-terma
#| lst-cap: "Term A: A - tr(RIV) reduces to 0 (symbolic)."
A = trace_normal(Trace(I_mis * I_obs.inv()), n)         # (L3) with (L1)
assert sp.expand(A - trRIV) == 0
print("A        =", A, "  -> A - tr(RIV) =", sp.expand(A - trRIV))
```

**Term B** $= -\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ — plug L2 into L4; the $n$ ($=k$)
terms cancel, leaving **no** dimension constant (@eq-termB; this is the cancellation the
Term B / L2 disambiguation in the body warns about):

```{python}
#| label: lst-mc-termb
#| lst-cap: "Term B: B + tr(RIV)/2 reduces to 0, no leftover -k/2 (symbolic)."
B = trace_normal(sp.Rational(-1, 2) * Trace(I_com * (I_obs.inv() - I_com.inv())), n)
assert sp.expand(B + trRIV / 2) == 0
print("B        =", B, "  -> B + tr(RIV)/2 =", sp.expand(B + trRIV / 2))
```

**Combine** $T = A + B = +\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ (@eq-combine, @eq-theorem):

```{python}
#| label: lst-mc-combine
#| lst-cap: "Combine: T = A + B = tr(RIV)/2, exact (symbolic)."
T = trace_normal(A, n) + B
assert sp.expand(T - trRIV / 2) == 0
print("T = A+B  =", sp.expand(T))
print("T - tr(RIV)/2 =", sp.expand(T - trRIV / 2), " <- exact zero")
```

### MVN Fisher-information closed forms (symbolic, $p=2$) {#sec-verify-mvn}

Block structure of $I_{\text{com}}$ (D15): block-diagonal on $(\mu,\operatorname{vech}\Sigma)$
with $I_\mu = N\Sigma^{-1}$ and $I_{\operatorname{vech}\Sigma} = \tfrac{N}{2}D_p^\top(\Sigma^{-1}\otimes\Sigma^{-1})D_p$.

```{python}
s11, s12, s22, Nsym = sp.symbols('sigma11 sigma12 sigma22 N', positive=True)
Sig2 = sp.Matrix([[s11, s12], [s12, s22]])
Sig2_inv = Sig2.inv()
D2 = duplication_matrix(2, sym=True)
I_mu_2 = Nsym * Sig2_inv
I_sig_2 = sp.Rational(1, 2) * Nsym * D2.T * sp.Matrix(
    sp.kronecker_product(Sig2_inv, Sig2_inv)) * D2
I_sig_2 = sp.simplify(I_sig_2)   # safe here: ordinary Matrix, not Trace
I_mu_2, I_sig_2
```

### NumPy reimplementations checked against R {#sec-verify-numpy}

These mirror `fisher_info_com_mvn`, `fisher_info_obs_mvn`, `loglik_mvn`, and
`barQ_fiml_analytic` in `verification/00-setup.R`. Matching the R outputs on a fixed
replicate is the gate that the symbolic construction equals the code the W1 simulations
actually ran.

```{python}
def fisher_com(mu, Sig, N):
    p = len(mu); Si = inv(Sig); D = duplication_matrix(p)
    kk = p + p * (p + 1) // 2; I = np.zeros((kk, kk))
    I[:p, :p] = N * Si
    I[p:, p:] = (N / 2) * D.T @ np.kron(Si, Si) @ D
    return I

def fisher_obs(Sig, R):
    p = Sig.shape[0]; kk = p + p * (p + 1) // 2
    vi = vech_index_table(p); I = np.zeros((kk, kk))
    pats = {}
    for r in range(R.shape[0]):
        key = tuple(R[r].astype(int)); pats[key] = pats.get(key, 0) + 1
    for key, npat in pats.items():
        Oi = [i for i in range(p) if key[i] == 0]; q = len(Oi)
        if q == 0:
            continue
        Sooi = inv(Sig[np.ix_(Oi, Oi)])
        for a in range(q):
            for b in range(q):
                I[Oi[a], Oi[b]] += npat * Sooi[a, b]
        Dq = duplication_matrix(q)
        Isub = 0.5 * Dq.T @ np.kron(Sooi, Sooi) @ Dq
        full = np.array([p + vi[Oi[a], Oi[b]] - 1
                         for b in range(q) for a in range(b, q)])
        I[np.ix_(full, full)] += npat * Isub
    return I

def loglik(mu, Sig, X):
    Si = inv(Sig); _, ld = slogdet(Sig); hl = 0.5 * np.log(2 * np.pi); p = len(mu)
    d = X - mu
    return float(np.sum(-p * hl - 0.5 * ld - 0.5 * np.einsum('ij,jk,ik->i', d, Si, d)))

def barQ_fiml(mu_e, Sig_e, mu_i, Sig_i, Y, R):
    Np, p = Y.shape
    Sei = inv(Sig_e); _, ld = slogdet(Sig_e); hl = 0.5 * np.log(2 * np.pi)
    total = 0.0; pats = {}
    for r in range(Np):
        key = tuple(R[r].astype(int)); pats.setdefault(key, []).append(r)
    for key, rows in pats.items():
        Mi = [i for i in range(p) if key[i] == 1]
        Oi = [i for i in range(p) if key[i] == 0]
        if not Mi:                              # fully observed
            for r in rows:
                d = Y[r] - mu_e
                total += -p * hl - 0.5 * ld - 0.5 * d @ Sei @ d
        elif not Oi:                            # all missing
            d = mu_i - mu_e; tr = np.trace(Sei @ Sig_i)
            total += len(rows) * (-p * hl - 0.5 * ld - 0.5 * d @ Sei @ d - 0.5 * tr)
        else:                                   # partial: Schur conditional
            Sooi = inv(Sig_i[np.ix_(Oi, Oi)]); Smo = Sig_i[np.ix_(Mi, Oi)]
            condV = Sig_i[np.ix_(Mi, Mi)] - Smo @ Sooi @ Smo.T
            tr = np.trace(Sei[np.ix_(Mi, Mi)] @ condV)
            for r in rows:
                cm = mu_i[Mi] + Smo @ Sooi @ (Y[r, Oi] - mu_i[Oi])
                Xh = np.zeros(p)
                for o in Oi:
                    Xh[o] = Y[r, o]
                for jj, mm in enumerate(Mi):
                    Xh[mm] = cm[jj]
                d = Xh - mu_e
                total += -p * hl - 0.5 * ld - 0.5 * d @ Sei @ d - 0.5 * tr
    return total
```

The fixture `proof-verification-fixture.json` is one W1 replicate (N=500, non-monotone
MAR) plus the `W1-sweep-prod` cell aggregates, emitted by `verification/W1-sympy-fixture.R`.

```{python}
cands = [Path("proof-verification-fixture.json"),
         Path("manuscript/proof-verification-fixture.json")]
fix = next(p for p in cands if p.exists())
J = json.load(open(fix))

def as_float(x):
    try:
        return float(x)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return np.nan       # jsonlite serializes missing entries of Y as "NA"

p, k, N = J["meta"]["p"], J["meta"]["k"], J["meta"]["N"]
X = np.array(J["data"]["X"], float)
Y = np.array([[as_float(v) for v in row] for row in J["data"]["Y"]])
R = np.array(J["data"]["R"], float)
mu_obs = np.array(J["theta_obs"]["mu"], float)
Sig_obs = np.array(J["theta_obs"]["Sigma"], float)
mu_com = np.array(J["theta_com"]["mu"], float)
Sig_com = np.array(J["theta_com"]["Sigma"], float)
I_com_R = np.array(J["fisher"]["I_com"], float)
I_obs_R = np.array(J["fisher"]["I_obs"], float)
sc = J["scalars"]; cache = J["cache"]
print(f"replicate: p={p} k={k} N={N}, missing entries={int(np.isnan(Y).sum())}")
```

**Fisher information and tr(RIV) match R** (D15, @eq-trriv):

```{python}
#| label: lst-mc-fisher
#| lst-cap: "MVN I_com, I_obs, tr(RIV) match 00-setup.R to machine precision (D15)."
I_com_py = fisher_com(mu_obs, Sig_obs, N)
I_obs_py = fisher_obs(Sig_obs, R)
trRIV_py = float(np.trace(inv(I_obs_py) @ I_com_py) - k)

err_com = np.max(np.abs(I_com_py - I_com_R))
err_obs = np.max(np.abs(I_obs_py - I_obs_R))
print(f"max|I_com_py - I_com_R| = {err_com:.2e}")
print(f"max|I_obs_py - I_obs_R| = {err_obs:.2e}")
print(f"tr(RIV) py = {trRIV_py:.12f}  | R = {sc['trRIV_replicate']:.12f}"
      f"  | diff = {trRIV_py - sc['trRIV_replicate']:.2e}")
assert err_com < 1e-8 and err_obs < 1e-8
assert abs(trRIV_py - sc["trRIV_replicate"]) < 1e-8
```

**Information identity and $I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}\succeq 0$** (D7, @eq-info-identity):

```{python}
#| label: lst-mc-psd
#| lst-cap: "Information identity holds and I_mis|obs is PSD at the fixture (D7)."
I_mis_py = I_com_py - I_obs_py
print("max|I_com - (I_obs + I_mis)| =",
      f"{np.max(np.abs(I_com_py - (I_obs_py + I_mis_py))):.2e}")
print("min eigenvalue of I_mis|obs  =", f"{eigvalsh(I_mis_py).min():.4e}  (>= 0)")
assert eigvalsh(I_mis_py).min() > -1e-8
```

**FIML Q-function and the A / B / T decomposition match R** (D9, D10-D12):

```{python}
#| label: lst-mc-fiml
#| lst-cap: "Analytic FIML Q-function and A/B/T match 00-setup.R (D9-D12)."
barL_py = barQ_fiml(mu_obs, Sig_obs, mu_obs, Sig_obs, Y, R)
ell_com_at_com_py = loglik(mu_com, Sig_com, X)
ell_com_at_obs_py = loglik(mu_obs, Sig_obs, X)
A_py = barL_py - ell_com_at_obs_py
B_py = ell_com_at_obs_py - ell_com_at_com_py
T_py = barL_py - ell_com_at_com_py

for name, py, r in [("barL_obs", barL_py, sc["barL_obs"]),
                    ("A_r", A_py, sc["A_r"]),
                    ("B_r", B_py, sc["B_r"]),
                    ("T_r", T_py, sc["T_r"])]:
    print(f"{name:9s} py = {py:14.8f}  | R = {r:14.8f}  | diff = {py - r:.2e}")
    assert abs(py - r) < 1e-6
```

**$\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ within Monte-Carlo SE of $\mathbb{E}[T]$**
(@eq-theorem against the W1 production cache):

```{python}
#| label: lst-mc-mcse
#| lst-cap: "1/2 tr(RIV) sits within the MCSE band of the W1 cache E[T]."
half_pred_rep = 0.5 * trRIV_py            # from this replicate's theta_obs
half_pred_mc = cache["half_trRIV_pred"]   # 0.5 * MC-mean of analytic tr(RIV)
T_hat, T_mcse = cache["T_hat"], cache["T_mcse"]
lo, hi = T_hat - 1.96 * T_mcse, T_hat + 1.96 * T_mcse

print(f"cache E[T]            = {T_hat:.4f}  +/- {T_mcse:.4f} (MCSE)")
print(f"95% MCSE band         = [{lo:.4f}, {hi:.4f}]")
print(f"1/2 tr(RIV) [MC mean] = {half_pred_mc:.4f}")
print(f"1/2 tr(RIV) [replicate] = {half_pred_rep:.4f}")
print(f"cache E[A] = {cache['A_hat']:.3f} +/- {cache['A_mcse']:.3f}"
      f"  vs tr(RIV)_MC = {cache['trRIV_mean']:.3f}")
print(f"cache E[B] = {cache['B_hat']:.3f} +/- {cache['B_mcse']:.3f}"
      f"  vs -1/2 tr(RIV)_MC = {-half_pred_mc:.3f}")
assert lo <= half_pred_mc <= hi
print("\nGATE PASSED: 1/2 tr(RIV) lies within the MCSE band of E[T].")
```

### Term A mechanism: the conditional-variance penalty {#sec-verify-termA-mech}

The $+\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ of Term A (@sec-termA) enters the FIML Q-function
through the per-observation conditional-variance penalty
$-\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\Sigma_{\text{eval}}^{-1}\bar V_i)$, where for a partial
pattern $V = \Sigma_{MM} - \Sigma_{MO}\Sigma_{OO}^{-1}\Sigma_{OM}$ is the Schur
complement (A7). Shown symbolically for a 3-variable pattern, $X_1$ missing:

```{python}
syms = sp.symbols('s11 s12 s13 s22 s23 s33', positive=True)
s11, s12, s13, s22, s23, s33 = syms
Sig3 = sp.Matrix([[s11, s12, s13], [s12, s22, s23], [s13, s23, s33]])
Mi, Oi = [0], [1, 2]
Smm = Sig3[Mi, Mi]; Smo = Sig3[Mi, Oi]; Soo = Sig3[Oi, Oi]
V = sp.simplify(Smm - Smo * Soo.inv() * Smo.T)     # Schur complement, scalar here
print("conditional Var(X_1 | X_2, X_3) =")
sp.pprint(V[0, 0])
```

The pattern-sum-to-$\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ reduction is a conditional-expectation
calculation Sympy does not carry out cleanly; it is verified numerically (`A_py` matches R
to machine precision in @lst-mc-fiml, and the cache confirms $\mathbb{E}[A]\approx\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$).

### Tier B — abstract generalization (MVN-free) {#sec-verify-tierB}

The trace bookkeeping above used abstract `MatrixSymbol` objects with no MVN commitment,
so $T = +\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ already holds for any regular likelihood
given L1-L4. What remains MVN-free to verify is that the score-equation expansion delivers
the covariance input L2 (@eq-varGap): the observed score is the conditional expectation of
the complete-data score under MAR, so $\mathbb{E}[U_{\text{com}}U_{\text{obs}}^\top]=I_{\text{obs}}$.

```{python}
# (L2) from the efficient-estimator orthogonality, symbolically.
Vobs = I_obs.inv()                                  # Var(theta_obs)        (L1)
Vcom = I_com.inv()                                  # Var(theta_com)
Cov_oc = (I_obs.inv() * I_obs * I_com.inv()).doit() # Cov(obs,com) = I_com^{-1}
print("Cov(theta_obs, theta_com) =", Cov_oc, " (= I_com^{-1} = Var(theta_com))")
Vdiff = symmetric_reduce(sp.MatAdd(Vobs, -Cov_oc, -Cov_oc.T, Vcom).doit())
target = (I_obs.inv() - I_com.inv()).doit()
print("Var(diff) - (I_obs^{-1} - I_com^{-1}) =", sp.expand(symmetric_reduce(Vdiff - target)))
```

Feeding the symbolic L2 straight back into Term B and recombining — no separately-asserted
covariance, a pure consequence of the score relations (L2 @eq-varGap):

```{python}
#| label: lst-mc-tierb
#| lst-cap: "Tier B: L2 derived from the score relations recombines to tr(RIV)/2 (MVN-free)."
B_from_scores = trace_normal(sp.Rational(-1, 2) * Trace(I_com * Vdiff), n)
A_from_L1 = trace_normal(Trace(I_mis * Vobs), n)
T_B = trace_normal(A_from_L1, n) + B_from_scores
assert sp.expand(T_B - trRIV / 2) == 0
print("Tier B:  T = A + B =", sp.expand(T_B), " == tr(RIV)/2  [exact]")
```

### Verification summary {#sec-verify-summary}

| Identity | Status | Listing |
|---|---|---|
| $\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV}) = \operatorname{tr}(I_{\text{obs}}^{-1}I_{\text{com}}) - k$ | symbolic | @lst-mc-trriv |
| Term A $= +\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ (given L1, L3) | symbolic | @lst-mc-terma |
| Term B $= -\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ (given L2, L4) | symbolic | @lst-mc-termb |
| $T = A + B = +\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ | symbolic | @lst-mc-combine |
| MVN $I_{\text{com}}, I_{\text{obs}}, \operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ | numeric (machine precision) | @lst-mc-fisher |
| Information identity, $I_{\text{mis}\mid\text{obs}}\succeq 0$ | numeric | @lst-mc-psd |
| FIML Q-function, $A/B/T$ | numeric (machine precision) | @lst-mc-fiml |
| $\tfrac12\operatorname{tr}(\mathrm{RIV})$ vs $\mathbb{E}[T]$ | within MCSE | @lst-mc-mcse |
| Tier B (abstract, MVN-free): L2 from scores | symbolic | @lst-mc-tierb |

---

### References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
